reviews.review_description:
If you play seated and you happen to 
drop something, forget about it...
So did developers old game dread Halls was kind of the go-to horror game for a while but this one took it to another level completely.

One of my favorite VR Horror games of all times. This game still scares me every time I play it even years later. May I please request a CO-OP version of this game in the future. This game is already great on its own but CO-OP would being a whole other level to this game if you can drag your friends in the horror adventure too. Thank you for making this game!￼￼￼￼

reviews.review_title: Poor execution of a good concept
reviews.review_description:
Making a game overly difficult doesn’t mean you’ve achieve an atmosphere of “dread,” sometimes you’ve just frustrated players too early for them to continue playing.
Where to begin...
1.	You don't enough oxygen and are constantly looking for more.  Sometimes you won’t find enough and will die before doing anything besides looking for more oxygen.
2.	You don't have a room-labeled map and the map you do have only shows you rooms you've already been in.  This means you have no idea where you’re going and have to blindly explore everything, all while running out of oxygen.
3.	Your starting weapon (whatever it may be) only stuns the enemies at best and that is only after using multiple rounds of ammo (which is scarce).
4.	The spaceship you're exploring apparently runs exclusively on batteries(?!) that you must swap between locations in order to accomplish anything.  They also drain quickly and cannot be recharged (that I’m aware of).
5.	You move as if both legs are broken while enemies move very quickly, some enemies jump across entire rooms so fast you can barely follow them.
In short, this game isn’t appealing and it isn’t fun.  There isn’t even a backstory or context to events leading into the game for players to care about.  This really just feels like lazy game development in my opinion, but I’m sure there are plenty of gamers who would enjoy the straight: “space shooter with multiple ticking-clocks and lack of anything resembling a story or narrative” approach.

Its scary, its challenging, and it doesn't hold your hand. And with that comes the joy of adventure, taking risks and growing as a player. If you want a genuine survival experience that rewards and pushes you to try again and again, I highly recommend this game!

As a gamer I must say, Cosmodread is where it’s at. The scariest, peek around the corner, pop out scare of a lifetime. It’s literally feels like a blanket of scary dread lingers in the ship. Totally neat.
One of my favorite games on vr. It actually has a lot of replayibility. Finally a game with some meat on its bones for vr

First, lemme get this out the way: my review is a work in progress. 

Install N° Uno: ~795MB later, Im hyped up to launch this thing, thinking about how everyone else says the graphics look ugly, but I played the ASCII variants of Rogue and BRogue, how are "dated" graphics gonna stop ME?! I launch about a minute after the final install, get the generic "Disturbing Content" warning, get the game splash screen with "Loading..." on it, then Im plunged into the dark Abyss with only sound. Not sure if a menu was supposed to pop up immediately or not, but with a sigh of resignation I uninstalled. I now wait for Install N° 2 to finish, I hope it gets it right (and Meta needs to go the way of Steam and add the ability to verify install files, my goodness).

Install N° 2: I finally got the main menu!! Yay!! Snap turn and teleport movement off the rip? You have now assumed too much. I open my menu, I tweak my settings to give me smooth locomotion and smooth camera turning, and I give my controls a test to see if they hold water... they do, but apparently if I turn to quick with the right analog stick Im plunged back into the Abyss. No biggie, I'll just relaunch the game... it happened AGAIN when I turned my right analog stick again. I CANNOT play games that restrict me to snap turning and teleport move just to satiate the requirements to run the game. You need to issue a patch.

I just bought and tried the game for about 15 minutes. It seems like a good game except for one very big thing. Like many games of this kind, if you play in sitting mode and an object falls to the ground, you can no longer reach it. So either you set a key to be able to bend down or you increase the interaction radius to be able to interact with the object in question. If this is not resolved, I will be forced to request a refund before the time runs out. 3 stars for now.

My first time playing this game I entered a pitch black room, where I then heard a noise on the ceiling, and was greeted by a flying human torso molded to resemble a cuttlefish. It flew down to me, and we stared at each other at eye level for a while. Confused and not knowing how to proceed I waved at it, that's when it headbutted me and scared the living crap out of me. I shot at it dazed and confused missing a ton of shots as it threw scrap metal at me with telekinesis. Eventually I took it down however and continued my adventure. 10/10

On a more informational note. The atmosphere, enemy/sound design all that are top notch. The environment is cramped and your injured slow character solidifies that. And the progression and story are great. The only thing that grinds my gears are the fact that there are a few weapons that do it all better. Each weapon should have a purpose/strength and weakness. I won't spoil the weapons but while some weapons follow this a couple of them just suck or are OP.

This game, and Dreadhalls, are the best VR Roguelites available on the Quest. It can be a little frustrating when you get a bad map and no ammo anywhere. But keep getting the blueprints, expand your options game by game. I bought this game on the first Quest and just beat it on my new Quest 3, and it was a great experience. Absolutely begging the developer to make something new, but exactly like the other 2.

reviews.review_title: Test of wit, survival and skill
reviews.review_description: This game takes things to a whole new level when it comes to survival, having to manage your oxygen and resources as well as your willingness to go into room rooms where there might be lurking danger, the more you play the easier tends to get as you develop a strategy I don’t wanna give out too many hints, but resource management is key and having a plan of action to explore the ship is your next thing if you like a challenge, this is a great game that does that and I enjoy every minute of it. Something that I think that would make the game a little more fun as if there was maybe a co-op mode but no for those of you that have played resources are a deal but all in all I think it would be fun. All I have to say is watch out for the cosmanaut
It was the only VR game that scared me. 10/10, peak horror game.

Can you please add an easy mode  where you don't need oxygen?
Please 🙏

reviews.review_title: One of the best games for VR
reviews.review_description:
The setting is creepy and immersive, the combat is fun and involves a fair amount of strategy. The overall setting, enemies and the objectives for getting the ship started remind me a lot of Dead Space, just enough to be its own game without ripping it off. The procedurally generated rooms and loot is a lot like Binding of Isaac or other rogue-lite games. If you like those sorts of games, definitely pick this one up!

*My only complaint is that sometimes it crashes, seemingly at random, and you lose some progress. Doesn't happen often enough to be a big deal but it is irritating when it does happen.

In my opinion is cosmodread a bit overrated. Its not bad but not really special either. It feels a bit like the vr game Genotype, but that game is way better than this one. I Dont like the game dynamics so much and miss difficulty options. Graphics on quest 3 are subpar.

This game is engaging, intriguing, intense, and immersive (seriously, don't play in a close space or you'll end up breaking something the first time you try to leap away from a baddie with your IRL body). It's got some really good mechanics too, like being able to look at your wrist for a mini-map or reach over your non-dominant shoulder for fresh ammo. I haven't uncovered a lot of the story yet, but like I said, it's intriguing. I even like the crafting mechanics. There's also a near constant state of tension as you explore the various rooms and fight monsters, and when you successfully survive an encounter with a teleporting stack of flesh and teeth, you feel like a god.

There are some problems, or else I'd give it 5/5. The biggest, and worth a star off by itself, is the lightning gun, or at least the fact you can get stuck with that gun as your only "weapon". Everything about it stinks, except that you can recharge it by pumping the handle, and even that is 2 seconds of anemic firing for 10 seconds of frantic pumping. The worst thing is the 10ft arc (already well inside the range of several monsters) that actually only HURTS things at 4ft or less. If it were just a hold-out gun, it'd be ok, but the RNG can saddle you with it as your main weapon, which basically ends that playthru before it begins, so one star off.

Some minor gripes: The character's pacing is terminally slow (could really have used a "pump your arms to sprint" feature). Sometimes damage comes from nowhere bcz the monster animation donked out or it was in your blindspot. Health packs can be partially used (yay), but partially used health packs can't go back in your inventory (boo) so what's even the point? It's easy to overlook the fact that monsters don't stay dead unless you light up the room they're in.

Still, overall great game. Full of tension. Lots of good mechanics. Rewards skill and alertness. Neat story elements. Could use some tweaks, and the lighting gun belongs in the trash.

The graphics are unreasonably too low compared to the pc version or other quest 2 games. Raw polygons and low quality blurry lines which remind me of the early 3d games. It kills the immersion and the atmosphere. It definetely needs a graphics update.

Maybe this game used to be good, but on the Quest 3, this game is very dated, textures are very low quality.  Either they need to update it or put it in the bargain bin, not at all worth paying full price for.

To the game creators, if you want to actually put some more love into your game, I’ll change the review.  Otherwise you really have no place on modern headsets.

reviews.review_title: The RNG is the real enemy
reviews.review_description:
This game makes an outstanding first impression. It might be worth it just for that.

But as a game, it feels like it's all down to whether the RNG is in your favor for the current run. Enemy placement, enough oxygen canisters to take it slow, enough ammo and resources to clear out particularly tough rooms. Are the necessary energy cells in reach or being held hostage by the strongest monsters?

If you play enough failed runs, you'll probably end up with a store of blueprints and options to take it slow and beat the game once. It can be tense a few times to be that careful, but feels frustrating to lose all that time after enough repetition.

I beat this for the first time last night. I would say it was a lucky run. Didn't need any keycards besides the starter blue key, I avoided the game's worst monsters entirely, got just enough health boosters, and had only just enough oxygen to get to the last objective.

Ok, so the graphics could be better, but the game's atmosphere absolutely, totally makes up for it.  Everything this game sets out to do, it does very well...  Exploration, anticipation, tension...

This is NOT for the run & gun type of player.  You are vulnerable, underpowered and have to figure out what to do/how to do it over multiple attempts, which is genuinely useful and rewarding.

Even if each run is similar, there's surprising depth to the gameplay and how you can go about things.  Once you've successfully completed a few runs, you can then spice things up with modifiers.  And it's kept me trying again... And again...
Sucks massive balls  do not buy
This game is highly immersive, fun, with a lot of replay value I keep finding myself going back to it again and again. It’s a game that has a very likeable charm about it if your into sci-if horror.

I really want to like this game but it is too hard. I keep encountering enemies that kill me before my puny gun can kill them. I have unlocked a number of modifiers, but every one of them so far makes it even harder.

The rest of the game is excellent. I will keep trying, for a while at least, but it could really do with an easier mode, or a better gun. I hate it when Devs forget many people are not caffeine addicted hardcore gamers.

Great game, great atmosphere, tough to beat, love the design and monsters. I hope there is a second or extended version sometime. I give it very positive reviews, love playing it, searching, surviving.

If you're looking for a terrifying survival game here it is. Look through every cabinet looking for ammo or supplies while in a very scary atmosphere, fend for your life as creatures come at you, and dying kind of has a point. The whole game is very well made and well worth the 15$ I spent

reviews.review_title: Even if you play for just 30 minutes...
reviews.review_description:
I played this game the moment I saw the Rouge lite elements in the game. The audio design, for most part, is very well done. Some of the audio design is passable, at its best. 

But the graphics, oh my God the graphics. I'm not going to say it's anything like Half-Life: Alyx, but that's part of its charm. The first monsters I ran into I legitimatey couldn't get away and shut the door fast enough, and I dashed away physically and ran into my dinner table. 

Do you have any idea what kind of visceral fear you have to instill in order to cause someone to run into their table and scream like a girl? And it wasn't even a jumpscare, the environment and buildup to just witnessing the monster was enough to do that to me. 

Buy the game. I absolutely promise it's worth the buy.
I could only last about an hour and a half before I needed to take a break just to breath.

reviews.review_title: Good game for a niche audience
reviews.review_description:
This game is like having a digital frienemy. You’ll love it and hate it equally. 

I love games like dead space and xcom, and it has the feel of dead space with the difficulty arc of xcom (things won’t end well until you get over certain blueprint humps).

I enjoy that you basically have to figure out everything on your own from scratch. Even YouTube videos are rare to explain what to do. 

A lot of reviews are very positive about the audio, but I think it is overstated. There seems to be 2 audio types in the game - environment and creature. Environmental audio sounds amazing but if you pay attention it is on a different level of directional audio. Basically, when you hear directional audio perfectly clear, it is just an environmental aspect to be spooky and should be disregarded. The creature audio is poorly directional and it really needs to be on the same level. When you deal with something like crawlers, audio will not be much help finding them as the jump around. It would be cool if powering lights would cause a directional shrike to any live enemies in the room. 

I have a lot of problems with the gesture to pull ammo from my shoulder area. The hit box is too far back to me and it rarely successfully grabs on the first try. Please fix this to be more forgiving. I die a lot now due to failing to retrieve ammo. 

I haven’t found too many bugs, but once I crafted a laser sight, attached it to a pistol, it never worked and eventually disappeared. On many occasions I try to carry an item in my left hand for a length of time (as an extra item slot) and the item disappeared at some point and can’t be found retracing your path. Sometimes it is hard to get yourself close enough to grab an item. Once close enough to be highlighted the grab trigger should bring it to your hand. I have regularly had issues trying to grab items right in front of me. I have other games and never had similar grabbing problems. 

I am a bit obsessed at getting better runs but after I beat it, I’ll probably just bring it out around Halloween. The game touts having a different map each run, but it always breaks down into a base format of 4 branches off the blue key card room, where each branch is a path to 1 key room. 

With some updates that address the issues I mentioned it would be a 5 star game.

This game is very atmospheric and has wonderful sound design, although the graphics are somewhat lacking you don’t notice it when you’re running for your life after being jump-scared by a disappearing headless alien. I can say it’s definitely scary enough to make you jump a couple of times until you get used to it after a few runs. Happy this was my first horror vr game I had a lot of fun.
Very enjoyable and the price you can't beat for the content

So I have only been in the vr space for a couple months now but have played many titles. Cosmodread is for sure in my top 5. Its really not a game you can play for hours, just because its pretty terrifying; you end up scaring yourself most of the time. Devs really did make and awesome rogue like game that had me leaving my play sessions shaking and with my heart pumping out my chest.

Every time you start a round the map I different and I think this is the biggest reason I like this game. It is as if everything I play its a new game. However the weapons and ammo are to few and too weak with no major upgrades as creatures get stronger the deeper you get.

I say 4 star because of the lack of firepower and ammo and lack of lootable items. But also due to movement. Having O2 being a time table on how fast you need to move to get things done, the movement speed is just to slow and I tend to lose interest after running around the map for long periods. The inventory space also is just too small for the amount of things you need to scavenge in order to create weapons, health, ammo, O2, or anything else.

I am looking forward to seeing what updates will be coming...if any.


UPDATE!!!!!

Changing my rating as I can't seem to play the game anymore. It just say syncing and won't let me open the game. I have tried restarting my oculus even went as far as uninstalling and reinstalling the game, knowing this would delete all my game data. I didn't want to do this but to play the game I made the effort. Unfortunately, this did not change anything. At first it wouldn't let me reinstall through the oculus, but I managed to reinstall through my phone app. Sadly though, thus made no difference with the syncing. 

I am really upset over the fact that I can no longer play this game even more so the money I have lost by purchasing this game that will not even start.

developer_response.body: It seems the Oculus Cloud system can sometimes get stuck in some devices. Please try uninstalling the game, then rebooting the headset before installing it again. Another option might be a factory reset of the device. But please contact Oculus Support and ask them for what you can do to fix this issue.

reviews.review_description: I never have played a rogue like game before but I'm glad this is my first,  I love this game so much.  I play it every day since I still haven't beaten it but no matter how many times I fail I never get upset since it doesn't take long to really get back where you were when you restart.  The fact that every time you play the game shifts and changes is a cool addition. Finding data drives also just feels rewarding,  I feel more powerful the more I collect. This game does have its flaws,   the graphics on the quest 2 aren't the best I've seen on this platform but that's fine and....um the lightning gun kinda sucks....look I'm running out of things to say that are flaws,  this game is a good one and you should try it.

This game perfectly demonstrates why I hate roguelikes. When you boil it down there's maybe half an hour of content here, but when you randomize everything you may never complete it just due to bad luck. You play over and again, making no progress until the stars align and you stumble upon the resources you need when you need them. There are blueprints that do carry over that let you craft items, but they are extremely rare. After roughly 20 attempts I've only found 3. In those runs I've only managed to finish 2 objectives before I end up running out of oxygen, ammo, or healing items.
It's like Alien, Event Horizon, and Pandorum all rolled in one! A must for fans of Sci-fi Horror!

reviews.review_title: Best survival horror game for VR
reviews.review_description: Cosmodread is fun, scary and not repetitive. For 15 dollars it is worth every penny. The scares are always fresh beacuase there are new modifiers to change up your game experience and the rooms are randomized. 5 stars!!

I really didn't like the game at first. I even considered returning it. But after a fee times of playing it I got sucked in. This is the same thing darksouls did for me. It is an incredible game and I love it. I would like to see a co-op mode added.

reviews.review_title: A game with dynamic shadows. FINALLY!
reviews.review_description:
Graphics are good except for some textures being Uber low res. Maybe an update for quest 2?

Hand collision would be nice.

More entities and garbage.

My biggest gripe is some of the enemies look cartoon-like.

Gameplay is solid and fun.

Sound design is stellar.

It’s fun and delightfully unpredictable.
Glad I bought it but I wanna see these dreadhalls devs add more content and nuance. More lore.
Game is sweet and gets better and better as you get further and its the longest game ive bought so far so definitely worth the money.

reviews.review_title: Great game but I have a suggestion
reviews.review_description: I have just got it but I played it for a good bit and I am enjoying it the movement system is good and I love the fact that it has a sitting mode  however it is really difficult to aim the crossbow due to the fact that it has pretty much no iron sights

reviews.review_title: Amazing but with a few problems
reviews.review_description: Let me start this out by saying Cosmodread is already going to be one of my favorite horror games. The audio, the weapons, everything is so well done. Definitely worth the price, which I'm glad its low like Dreadhalls' price(Another fantastic horror game). I have experienced some glitches, however. I can hear monsters through walls, but that part might be intentional. The loading screens seem to glitch and leave me with a black screen. After I died I was left with a black screen and nothing loaded so I had to restart. Lastly, when things fall on the floor they are very difficult to pick up! The character just wouldn't crouch low enough, I had to wiggle my hand until it was highlighted so I could pick it up.

This game play amazing. The atmosphere is tense and the noises...I love this game and the devs who made it. I hope there’s more to come for cosmodread like  more weapons and/or  new monsters. This game has a lot of potential but is great as is.

If you get motion sick by games that have a lot of moving around, rather then standing/ sitting, this game isn’t for you. I played through it having to take breaks like ever 5/10 minutes because I felt so nauseous. It was worth playing for sure, but for people who have no tolerance, please be careful! :)
Game is nice and all but get kinda boring without anyone

Fantastic atmosphere which still gets my heart racing despite many hours of play.  It needs melee combat though and the ability to push past enemies (taking damage of course).   Also curious as to why there are objects on tables the same size as objects you can pick up but you can’t even move these other ones.  That ruins some of the immersion.  But aside from that, I love it.  Well worth the money.  Could do with better physics when you explode things near the creatures.  They should be blown apart or at least be sent flying.  Usually they just die normally, dropping straight down.  Also seems to be a glitch returning back to the loading shuttle or resuming a paused game where you can end up starting inside a wall.  Excellent.  Reminds me of System Shock.  Now THAT would make a great VR game too.
Pretty good game. Definitely recommend.

I wanted to love this game. A horror themed Roguelite is what made me buy it. However, you don’t get stronger after every run. I’ve done multiple runs and haven’t unlocked anything of note. Looks like you can find blueprints to unlock weapons, but, when I think of Roguelite, I think of upgrading damage, weapons, health, etc. This has none of that..
Movement is extremely slow, the idea is good however its just not my cup of tea.

This game is wonderful but I will go ahead and give some constructive criticism only because I love the game and want it to be even better.
       Things are often just a bit too far out of reach, and the infected rooms, the infection itself is a bit flat graphics wise.
      Honestly for $15 this game is a steal and I can't recommend it enough.

A true survival horror title filled with unforgettable moments/situations, powered by an excellent atmosphere and great rng. Explore, run, scream and die ! Cosmodread surely will have you going through a lot should you dare trying to beat it (°-°)
Awesome game but they need to add more paths to explore the game could be short if you speed run it.
I really enjoy how this game is different every time you play. Searching for items to heal you or to help you fight off monsters. This is a must buy.

reviews.review_title: It's going, but a warning for lefties...
reviews.review_description: Your flashlight is on your right hand. Very difficult to shoot straight with the wrong hand or see what you're doing if you shoot with the left hand. Also, you have to either put down your weapon to open doors or risk being blind by opening the door with the hand your light is on.

Something that's really important to me when it comes to Roguelike/Roguelite games is replayability. That sounds obvious, but it's surprising how many game creators miss the mark there. Before picking up Cosmodread I was worried about that replayability. If you've read anything about the game you'll see that it's not too terribly difficult to beat depending on your luck. I think overall that's a good thing because it keeps you from being frustrated. In my opinion, being frustrated in a horror game makes you less afraid of the actual horror.

I was one of the people who got lucky. I got the game yesterday, got a lucky blueprint drop on maybe my fourth run, and I beat the same save file today. If that was the end of it I would still be satisfied with my purchase and probably replay it a few times. However, I'm particularly excited to go back and that's because of the modifiers.

The modifiers don't seem to be talked about much in any reviews I can find online and I know this kept me on the fence on purchasing. If I got lucky and beat the game after a few tries I probably wouldn't want to play again so soon if the only modifiers were "half health" or "double enemies", but they're more nuanced than that and I'm going to list them here for anyone else who may be on the fence for that reason! (Though there are a couple I haven't unlocked yet!)

Broken Map: Your minimap won't display anything
Pacifist: No weapons will spawn in the map
Scarcity: Very little ammo will spawn in the map
????: Locked
???: Locked
Low Energy: Most rooms will be dark. Batteries are scarce
Cat & Mouse: I'm going to let this one be a surprise out of respect for the dev and the game.
Arachnophobia: All other monsters are replaced by crawlers. Many crawlers
The Fortress: The ship has a world-class defense system
???: Locked
Backpack: Increased inventory capacity. Reduced health
???: Locked
Botched Revival: Drastically reduced health

Random: Select one or more modifiers at random

I've only recently purchased my oculus quest 2 and I have lots of learning to do, this game is my first purchase and I'm not disappointed 😀! I already have a grasp of the mechanics and this game is challenging and scary fun! I can hear my wife laughing when I yell out in fear from turning around to find one of a few menacing creatures creeping up on me. I'm sure to have lots of fun from this game as I'll share the experience with friends and family. Hope to see more from the developers of this game!!!

reviews.review_title: Not a fan of horror games, HOWEVER
reviews.review_description:
ive never been a fan of horror games. Resident evil, outlast, silent hill, ive always tried them, then have been way too nervous to continue past the first part of the game. I just cant play them lmao
idk why i thought i should grab a VR horror game, but i did!! Probably because i like space, and this is on a starship. But let me tell you, this has become my favorite vr game.
I think the aspect of it being a Roguelike ties into that great. Im not afraid of dying as much, because i know thats how you progress through these types of games. You're not supposed to finish it your first run through, and thats fine!! You can explore and learn without that permanent edge to it. idk man, i just rly like this game

It’s a fun game. Easy to grasp and pretty detailed, however when you’re too low on bullets to kill one enemy and too low on health to take a hit to get out, no matter how big that corridor is, it’s a trap. Without being lucky enough to get the right blueprints, you’ll probably end up having to explore a new direction, blocked by enemies, and locked in a relatively safe area with limited air and no way to get bullets to progress. 

All that aside, it’s a good game.

Yknow. So you dont pee yourself.

Joking aside its not terrifying, but a huge feeling of tension and dread that doesnt go away.

The gameplay is fun, slow paced, and tense.
A shipwide puzzle survival game where youre probably not making it out alive. Extremely fun, sound is solid, and its got nearly infinite replayability.

The more you play, the easier it gets, but it nevef gets easy. Or medium. Each run is hard, and youre going to die empty handed cause its a roguelike.

This is a 5 star game with one star knocked off cause of frequent glitches with picking items up, disappearing items in the inventory that will force a save and reload, and a few enemy related glitches that will ruin even a perfect run.

Other than the game breaking inventory and acquisition glitches, theres very little done wrong in this game. It does what it does perfectly.

10/10 gameplay
3/10 optimization

Still 4 stars cause it works well when it works, but any time you try to pick something up youre anticipating this being the item that youll steuggle with for a minute before weeping that you really really need that ammo pack cause you shut a door on five monsters and youre gonna die now.

This is a must have, exploration survival experience for the quest!  What is around the next corner? What lurks in a room? Your task is to find a way back home from this stranded space ship. I look forward to discovering What has happened along the way!

Great game... 6 out of 5... would have paid a few bucks more for this, I would definitely pay for some DLC in the future.
